reasoning: The Lean statement is a direct, equivalent translation of the informal claim. `[CommRing R] [CommRing S]` encodes "R and S commutative"; `f : R →+* S` encodes the ring homomorphism; `P : Ideal S` with `Ideal.IsPrime P` encodes "P is a prime ideal of S"; and `Ideal.comap f P` is defined in Mathlib (Mathlib/RingTheory/Ideal/Maps.lean) exactly as the set-theoretic preimage `f ⁻¹' I` equipped with its ideal structure, matching "f^{-1}(P)". The conclusion `Ideal.IsPrime (Ideal.comap f P)` captures "is a prime ideal of R". No hypotheses are silently added or dropped; the quantifier structure and all types match the informal statement.
